explanation.text:
**The Logic:** CAGR stands for Compound Annual Growth Rate. It tells you the average annual rate at which your investment has grown over a specific period, assuming the growth was compounded each year.
**The Trap:** CAGR is not a ratio, return type, or report. It is specifically the compounded annual growth rate — a standardized way to compare investment performance across different time periods.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in Series V-A. CAGR is the most commonly used measure for periods longer than 1 year.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** CAGR is useful for comparing funds over the same period. If Fund A has a 3-year CAGR of 15% and Fund B has 12%, Fund A has grown faster on an annualized basis.
